top_signals.title: China can help end Ukraine war by pressuring Russia, U.S. Senator Graham says - Reuters
top_signals.summary: China can help end Ukraine war by pressuring Russia, U.S. Senator Graham says  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
top_signals.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions against First VPN Service (1VPNS) and its Ukrainian administrator for aiding ransomware groups. Separately, a Belarusian man was sanctioned for malware "cryptors."
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: America's Biggest Utility Sees Blackouts Ahead
top_signals.summary: The CEO of America’s largest utility, EXELON, foresees blackouts in the US in 2027. To boost power supply Calvin Butler wants states to allow his utility to build new power plants. Opponents will object, saying this would saddle utility consumers with the risks. Whereas now, the builders take the risks. Meanwhile, we are scratching our heads because economists have long theorized that utilities build excess rate base in order to raise earnings. We would amend this to argue that utility managers only deploy excess capital when they can earn…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: De Beers idles South Africa’s top diamond mine for two years
top_signals.summary: Cost-cutting measures deepen as the miner restructures operations and prepares for a long-awaited sale.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Activities of U.S. Affiliates of Foreign Multinational Enterprises, 2024
top_signals.summary: Majority-owned U.S. affiliates of foreign multinational enterprises employed 8.57 million workers in the United States in 2024, a 0.2 percent increase from 8.56 million workers in 2023, according to statistics on U.S. affiliate operations and finances released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis.
